items.title: What is Considered a Good Stock Dividend? 2 Healthcare Stocks That Fit the Bill.
items.author: Matt Dilallo
items.description: Research shows that dividend growth stocks deliver superior long-term returns compared to high-yield non-growing dividend stocks. Johnson & Johnson and Medtronic are highlighted as exemplary dividend stocks, both offering yields well above the S&P 500 average while maintaining decades-long records of consecutive annual dividend increases, supported by strong financial profiles and strategic growth investments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: JNJ is praised for its 2.2% dividend yield (nearly double S&P 500 average), 63 consecutive years of dividend increases (Dividend King status), AAA bond rating, strong free cash flow ($20B) covering dividends, and substantial R&D investments ($14.7B) supporting future growth.

MDT is commended for its 2.8% dividend yield, 48 consecutive years of annual dividend increases, healthy free cash flow ($5.2B) covering dividends, strong A-rated balance sheet, and continued R&D investment ($2.7B) with strategic acquisitions supporting dividend growth sustainability.
